title: Andinia masdevalliopsis
intro: Andinia masdevalliopsis is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Gesneriaceae. The health benefits of Andinia masdevalliopsis include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in relieving respiratory issues and skin infections. Andinia masdevalliopsis is traditionally used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and coughs.
taxonomy: Andinia masdevalliopsis is classified within the family Orchidaceae and is a genus of orchids. The species are primarily native to Central and South America. Andinia masdevalliopsis is also classified as a member of the subtribe Andiniinae. This subtribe is part of the tribe Maxillarieae.
